attribution_hypotheses.rationale: Tier 7.7l partitioned causal drivers improved Lorenz, Mackey-Glass, and repaired NARMA versus the matched single-pool reference.
attribution_hypotheses.required_probe: partition labels shuffled or merged should lose while full partitioned driver retains the gain.

attribution_hypotheses.rationale: The 7.7l candidate changed the causal driver basis; gains may come from lag/nonlinear features rather than partitioned recurrence.
attribution_hypotheses.required_probe: unpartitioned nonlinear/lag same-feature-budget control must be compared to the full candidate.

attribution_hypotheses.rationale: A larger or more useful interface can improve forecasting even if CRA-specific recurrence is not causal.
attribution_hypotheses.required_probe: same-feature-count random projection and readout-budget-matched controls must be included.

attribution_hypotheses.rationale: Tier 7.7l diversity-disabled was only 1.0165x worse than the full candidate, so diversity pressure is not yet a clean explanation.
attribution_hypotheses.required_probe: repeat diversity-disabled and include stronger no-partition/no-diversity controls before attributing to diversity.

attribution_hypotheses.rationale: Prior Lorenz gates showed generic/permuted bases can be competitive; attribution requires separating candidate-specific structure from generic projections.
attribution_hypotheses.required_probe: permuted recurrence, orthogonal basis, block basis, and same-feature random projection controls must remain locked.

attribution_hypotheses.rationale: Tier 7.7l target/time shuffles separated strongly, but all scoring gates must preserve these guards.
attribution_hypotheses.required_probe: target-shuffle and time-shuffle controls must fail strongly or the gate is blocked.

claim_boundary: Tier 7.7m is a pre-registration contract for attributing the Tier 7.7l partitioned-driver task gain. It does not implement the attribution gate, score a new model, promote a mechanism, freeze a baseline, claim public usefulness, or transfer anything to hardware/native runtime.

contract.decision_boundary: This contract authorizes only the Tier 7.7n attribution scoring gate. It does not authorize mechanism promotion, baseline freeze, hardware/native transfer, external-baseline superiority, broad public usefulness, language, AGI, or ASI claims.
contract.hypothesis: If causal driver partitioning is responsible, the full candidate should beat partition-shuffled, merged/unpartitioned, same-feature random projection, and generic-basis controls while coherent driver-group ablations produce interpretable losses.
contract.null_hypothesis: The 7.7l gain is explained by lag/nonlinear features alone, readout/interface budget, generic random basis effects, leakage, or non-reproducible scoring noise rather than CRA-specific partitioned drivers.
contract.question: What caused the Tier 7.7l partitioned-driver performance gain: causal driver partitioning, lag/nonlinear feature enrichment, readout/interface budget, diversity pressure, or a generic basis confound?

criteria.name: question locked
criteria.value: What caused the Tier 7.7l partitioned-driver performance gain: causal driver partitioning, lag/nonlinear feature enrichment, readout/interface budget, diversity pressure, or a generic basis confound?

criteria.name: hypothesis locked
criteria.value: If causal driver partitioning is responsible, the full candidate should beat partition-shuffled, merged/unpartitioned, same-feature random projection, and generic-basis controls while coherent driver-group ablations produce interpretable losses.

criteria.name: null hypothesis locked
criteria.value: The 7.7l gain is explained by lag/nonlinear features alone, readout/interface budget, generic random basis effects, leakage, or non-reproducible scoring noise rather than CRA-specific partitioned drivers.

driver_group_ablations.expected_if_causal: Loss on Lorenz and/or NARMA relative to full candidate.
driver_group_ablations.purpose: Measure whether short-timescale causal drivers carry the 7.7l gain.

driver_group_ablations.expected_if_causal: Loss on Mackey/NARMA or long-run Lorenz stability.
driver_group_ablations.purpose: Measure whether slow-timescale memory drivers carry the gain.

driver_group_ablations.expected_if_causal: Loss on Mackey and NARMA plus possible Lorenz degradation.
driver_group_ablations.purpose: Measure whether explicit causal lag channels explain the improvement.

driver_group_ablations.expected_if_causal: Loss on Lorenz and NARMA relative to linear partitioned control.
driver_group_ablations.purpose: Measure whether nonlinear basis enrichment explains the improvement.

driver_group_ablations.expected_if_causal: Specific, interpretable degradation without total collapse.
driver_group_ablations.purpose: Check whether one partition family dominates the result rather than a distributed mechanism.

nonclaims: not language, AGI, or ASI evidence

outcome_classes.allowed_claim: The 7.7l performance gain is attributable to causal driver partitioning under controlled software benchmark conditions.
outcome_classes.rule: Full partitioned driver beats partition-shuffled, merged/unpartitioned, same-feature random projection, and best generic sham by at least 5% on Lorenz; at least one coherent driver-group ablation hurts as predicted; Mackey/NARMA regressions stay within 10%; shuffle guards and compact regression pass.

outcome_classes.allowed_claim: The gain is useful but explained by causal lag/nonlinear feature enrichment, not partitioned CRA recurrence; no mechanism promotion until reframed.
outcome_classes.rule: Unpartitioned nonlinear/lag same-budget control matches full candidate, while removing lag/nonlinear drivers hurts.

outcome_classes.allowed_claim: The gain is an interface/readout-budget effect, not a CRA-specific mechanism; do not promote.
outcome_classes.rule: Same-feature-count random projection or widened-interface control matches or beats the full candidate.

outcome_classes.allowed_claim: Diversity pressure contributes causally to the observed performance improvement, subject to compact regression.
outcome_classes.rule: Full candidate materially beats diversity-disabled repeat and the difference is not explained by partition/feature/readout controls.

outcome_classes.allowed_claim: The observed improvement remains generic-basis explainable; no promotion.
outcome_classes.rule: Permuted, orthogonal, block, or random projection shams match or beat the candidate.

outcome_classes.allowed_claim: Keep as a diagnostic candidate; no promotion, freeze, hardware transfer, or broad usefulness claim.
outcome_classes.rule: Full candidate remains useful versus single-pool, but attribution controls disagree or margins are too small.

outcome_classes.allowed_claim: No attribution claim and no mechanism promotion.
outcome_classes.rule: Mackey/NARMA regress materially, target/time shuffles do not fail, or compact regression fails.

outcome_classes.allowed_claim: The 7.7l gain did not reproduce under attribution pressure; park the repair.
outcome_classes.rule: Full candidate no longer improves over single-pool/reference under the attribution matrix.

tasks.why: 7.7l produced the clearest repair signal here but did not prove the causal source.
7.7l improved Mackey versus single-pool; attribution should not destroy the established Mackey signal.
7.7l improved repaired NARMA versus single-pool; attribution must track whether lag/nonlinear drivers explain it.

variants.description: The Tier 7.7l repair candidate: partitioned causal drivers with lag/nonlinear channels and deterministic diversity pressure.
Keep feature budget and nonlinear/lag drivers but shuffle partition assignment so driver-to-state grouping should no longer be coherent.
Use the same causal drivers without fixed partitions to test whether partitioning itself matters.
Retain lag/nonlinear driver features at the same budget, but remove partition-specific recurrence/state grouping.
Keep partitions and lagged drivers but remove nonlinear transforms to test whether nonlinear enrichment is necessary.
Repeat the 7.7l diversity-disabled ablation under the attribution matrix.
Match feature count/readout budget with causal random projections but no CRA-specific partitioned driver structure.
Locked single-pool same-capacity reference with matched readout and training budget.
Preserve the prior generic recurrence sham family to test basis luck.
